name: Hoppin' John
recipeIngredient: 4 oz Black-eyed peas, picked over and rinsed
recipeInstructions:
"This traditional Southern dish is served on New Year's Day for good
luck throughout the year."
1. Spray a medium saucepan with nonstick cooking spray.
2. Add sausage (if using) and cook over medium heat, stirring occasionally, 3 minutes, until lightly browned.
3. Add onion and celery; cook, stirring until onion is golden.
4. Add garlic; cook, stirring, 1 minute more.
5. Add broth, black-eyed peas, bay leaf, and 2 cups water; bring to a boil.
6. Reduce heat, cover, and simmer for 1 hour, until peas are soft but not mushy.
7. Add rice, thyme, and pepper; cover and simmer for 15-20 minutes until rice is just tender.
8. Remove from heat; let stand covered for 10 minutes.
9. Sprinkle with scallions and parsley and serve.
Each serving (1-1/3 cup) with sausage provides:
1 V, 1-1/2 P, 1 B, 10 C
Per serving (with sausage):  281 cal, 17 g pro, 45 car,  4 g fat: 1 g poly, 1 g mono, 1 g sat;  302 mg sod, 19 mg chol.
Each serving (1-1/3 cup) without sausage provides:
1 V, 1/2 P, 1 B, 10 C
Per serving (without sausage):  238 cal, 13 g pro, 45 g car,  2 g fat: 0 g poly, 0 g mono, 0 g sat;  50 mg sod, 0 mg chol.
